analysis_result: ANOVA F = 5.3456, p = 0.001347 (scipy.stats.f_oneway across 5 PNST methylation clusters). MPNST_G1 has the highest mean fusions per sample (23.8), consistent with the paper's report.
denominator_used: N = 49 RNA-seq samples across 5 PNST clusters (MPNST_G1 n=6, MPNST_G2 n=7, PremalignantNF_G3 n=18, Benign_NF-G4 n=9, Sporadic_Spinal_NF_G5 n=9). Samples with no entry in MOESM8 assigned n_fusions=0.
assumptions_made: Mapped MOESM8 Sample_Id to MOESM4 RNA label as strings; counted fusion rows per sample; absent sample in MOESM8 => 0 fusions; restricted to MOESM4 rows with RNA-Seq=='X' and Methylation_Group in the 5 target clusters; used scipy.stats.f_oneway; group sizes (6,7,18,9,9) differ slightly from question-stated (6,6,19,10,8) but total n=49 matches.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM8 fusion table: 1031 rows, 19 columns. 82 unique Sample_Ids.
   - MOESM4 metadata: 108 rows, 15 columns (Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, RNA-Seq, RNA label).

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: MOESM4 rows where RNA-Seq == 'X' AND Methylation_Group in (MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2, PremalignantNF_G3, Benign_NF-G4, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5).
   - Final analytic set: n=49 RNA-seq samples across the 5 PNST clusters (actual MOESM4 counts: MPNST_G1 n=6, MPNST_G2 n=7, PremalignantNF_G3 n=18, Benign_NF-G4 n=9, Sporadic_Spinal_NF_G5 n=9).
   - NOTE: The question specifies group sizes (6, 6, 19, 10, 8) that differ slightly from the RNA-Seq=='X' subset in MOESM4 (6, 7, 18, 9, 9). Total (49) matches the paper's convention but n by group differs by 1 in some groups. We used the actual MOESM4 values.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Identifier mapping: MOESM8.Sample_Id -> MOESM4.'RNA label' (both cast to string).
   - Outcome: n_fusions per sample = count of MOESM8 rows grouped by Sample_Id; samples with no MOESM8 rows receive n_fusions=0.
   - 33 of 49 RNA-Seq samples had at least one fusion entry in MOESM8; the remaining 16 have n_fusions=0.

D. COMPUTATION
   - scipy.stats.f_oneway(*groups) across 5 methylation-cluster groups.
   - Group fusion counts (mean, n): MPNST_G1 23.83 (n=6); MPNST_G2 8.57 (n=7); PremalignantNF_G3 5.33 (n=18); Benign_NF-G4 5.78 (n=9); Sporadic_Spinal_NF_G5 11.78 (n=9).

E. RESULT
   - F = 5.3456
   - p = 0.001347
   - Direction: MPNST-G1 has the highest mean fusions per sample (23.8), consistent with the paper's claim.

F. CAVEATS
   - 16 MOESM4 samples had no entry in MOESM8; we imputed n_fusions=0 (i.e., assumed absence = 0 fusions, not missing data).
   - Group-size discrepancies vs the question-stated (6,6,19,10,8): likely a minor curation difference in which MOESM4 samples were deemed RNA-seq evaluable in the paper's figure; our counts (6,7,18,9,9) come directly from the RNA-Seq=='X' flag in MOESM4.
   - ANOVA assumes normality and equal variances; data show zero-inflation and variance heterogeneity (e.g., MPNST_G1 SD=18.7 vs Benign_NF-G4 SD=7.4). A Kruskal-Wallis test would be a more appropriate robustness check, but ANOVA is what was requested.
